title: narcissus asturiensis subsp. brevicoronatus
intro: Narcissus asturiensis subsp. brevicoronatus is a subspecies of the Narcissus asturiensis, a type of daffodil native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Narcissus asturiensis subsp. brevicoronatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying or extracting its flowers, leaves, or bulbs to create teas, infusions, or topical creams for medicinal use.
